---
author: Profe
title: Modelo de regresión
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)


library(plotly)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)

Salaries <- read_excel("Datos/Salaries.xlsx")
Salaries
View(Salaries)




```



# Aspecto gráfico

```{r}



grafico1<- ggplot(Salaries, aes(x = yrs.service, y = salary, color = sex) )  +
  geom_point()

ggplotly(grafico1)



```

Este gráfico balballablblablabalballab



# Tabla


```{r}

knitr::kable( head(Salaries,10) )

```




```{r}

rmarkdown::paged_table(Salaries)

```




```{r}
library(gt)
library(tidyverse)


Salaries %>%
  head(11) %>% 
  gt() %>% 
  tab_header(
    title = "Base de datos Salarios",
    subtitle = "Diplomado en gestión de datos"
  )
```




```{r}
library(DT)
datatable( Salaries)
```
